purpose: Upstream pipeline node for the BP-decomposition pass. Given an AWS implementation step within a BP, draft a binary promote / not_promote decision with rationale. Runs BEFORE any per-field authoring node — decides whether a principle exists for this step at all. Composed at runtime with system_prompts/generate.json.
system_addendum:
SECTION YOU ARE AUTHORING: step_promotion

Step_promotion is the BP-decomposition decision node. Unlike per-field generate nodes (statement, problem, solution, gates, etc.) which author content WITHIN an already-promoted principle, this node decides whether an AWS implementation step earns a principle at all. The decision is binary — promote or not_promote. There is no merge category.

WHEN A STEP IS PROMOTED

The step earns a standalone principle when it (a) concretises to a named artefact in the workload repository plus a CI gate or evidence check that can fire on it, (b) carries architectural content unique within the BP and not redundant with a sibling principle elsewhere, (c) sits in this BP / pillar rather than belonging to a different one, and (d) is a generic architectural mandate rather than a list of vendor service options to consider.

WHEN A STEP IS NOT PROMOTED

Four non-promotion reasons:

- decision_process_advice — the step is pure thinking input ('identify your minimum requirements', 'determine the candidates that clear the bar'). No commitable artefact, no CI-gateable check. The substance ends up as content INSIDE a sibling principle's authored fields (typically the step that promotes downstream of the thinking step).
- absorbed_by_sibling — the step's substance is restated or directly applied by another step / sibling principle. The substance lives inside that sibling's authored content.
- cross_pillar_scope — the substance belongs to a different pillar's BP (e.g. CloudTrail logging from a GENOPS Monitor BP belongs in GENSEC03 Event Monitoring). Documented as a gap with the target BP named; earmarked for the target walkthrough.
- vendor_menu — the AWS verbatim is dominated by service callouts ('consider Service X, explore Service Y, use Service Z'); no architectural mandate survives extraction.

Not_promoted does NOT mean the substance is lost. The catalogue's discipline is that absorbed substance lives inside a sibling principle's content (statement / solution / gates) as a content-authoring choice for that sibling's authoring nodes. That is NOT a third structural outcome of this decomposition pass; it is what the sibling decides when it authors its own fields.

DIMENSIONS YOUR DRAFT WILL BE SCORED ON

You will be scored against four dimensions (full rubric in sections/step_promotion/rubric.json):

1. has_enforceable_artefact — the decision correctly assesses whether a CI-gateable artefact exists.
2. architecturally_distinct — the decision correctly identifies whether the substance is unique within the BP.
3. in_bp_scope — the decision correctly places the substance in this BP or names the cross-pillar target.
4. not_vendor_menu — the decision correctly distinguishes a generic mandate from a vendor-service menu.

All four must score ≥ 2 to pass. Draft with these in mind from the start.

COMMON FAILURE MODES TO AVOID

- Promoting on a hollow gate. The artefact exists ('a requirements YAML', 'a config file') but the check is existence-only on a file trivially satisfied without real architectural rigour. Scores low on has_enforceable_artefact. Sharpen the gate to a content-shape check, or downgrade to not_promote.
- Generic non-promotion rationale ('too tactical', 'too high-level', 'better elsewhere'). Quote the AWS verbatim and name the specific non-promotion reason from the four shapes above.
- Promoting cross-pillar substance because it appears in this BP's verbatim. CloudTrail / GuardDuty in a GENOPS monitoring BP doesn't make them earn a GENOPS principle — they belong to GENSEC. Earmark, do not absorb.
- Treating 'consider X, explore Y, use Z' as a mandate by force-extracting it. If the AWS verbatim is dominated by vendor callouts and no generic rule survives stripping them, the call is not_promote on vendor_menu grounds.
- Scope creep on promote. Adding architectural content the AWS step does not carry (cost ceilings, regulatory constraints) when the step verbatim only says 'performance requirements'. Stay anchored on what AWS actually says.
user_template:
You are deciding the decomposition status of an AWS implementation step.

<aws_bp>
  <bp_code>{{ bp_code }}</bp_code>
  <bp_title>{{ bp_title }}</bp_title>
  <bp_implementation_guidance>{{ bp_implementation_guidance }}</bp_implementation_guidance>
  <pillar>{{ pillar }}</pillar>
  <focus_area>{{ focus_area }}</focus_area>
</aws_bp>

<step_under_decomposition>
  <step_number>{{ step_number }}</step_number>
  <step_title>{{ step_title }}</step_title>
  <step_verbatim_text>{{ step_verbatim_text }}</step_verbatim_text>
</step_under_decomposition>

<already_decided_siblings>
{{ sibling_decisions }}
</already_decided_siblings>

<adjacent_scope_candidates>
{{ adjacent_scope_candidates }}
</adjacent_scope_candidates>

Draft the decomposition decision for this step. Return the JSON object specified in the output contract.

schema.rationale: string — 2-6 sentences. Quote the AWS verbatim. Name the artefact + gate (when promote) or the non-promotion reason + absorbing principle / cross-pillar target (when not_promote).

if_promote.candidate_artefact: string | null — path-shaped (e.g. 'eval/strata.yaml', 'model-selection/decision.md'). Null when decision is not_promote.
if_promote.candidate_gate_shape: string | null — one-line sketch of the gate the principle would enforce. Null when decision is not_promote.

if_not_promote.non_promotion_reason: string | null — 'decision_process_advice' | 'absorbed_by_sibling' | 'cross_pillar_scope' | 'vendor_menu'. Null when decision is promote.
if_not_promote.absorbing_principle_id: string | null — sibling principle ID (e.g. 'GO1B1-04') when non_promotion_reason is 'absorbed_by_sibling'. Null otherwise.
if_not_promote.cross_pillar_target: string | null — target BP / focus area (e.g. 'GENSEC03 — Event monitoring') when non_promotion_reason is 'cross_pillar_scope'. Null otherwise.

example_passing_output.rationale: AWS step 1 verbatim: 'Identify the minimum performance requirements for a foundation model.' Pure thinking input to step 3's selection decision — work out the lowest bar before going shopping. No artefact, no CI-gateable check. The requirements end up as content inside the step 3 selection ADR (which IS promoted as GC1B1-01), so the substance is not lost. Same shape as GENOPS01-BP01 step 5 non-promotion.
